title: Why FIFA Won't Take Your Money (But Cryptocurrency Will)
excerpt: If your bank card is from a restricted country, FIFA's checkout page will reject you. But there's a payment method that doesn't care where you're from.
content:
The FIFA ticketing checkout doesn't accept all cards.

If your credit card was issued in Russia, Iran, North Korea, Venezuela, or a handful of other countries — the transaction won't process. The system blocks it before your bank even sees the request.

I know this because I watched it happen to a friend from Moscow. Entered his card details. Clicked pay. Error message. Three different cards. Same result.

## Why It Happens

FIFA's payment processor uses geolocation and issuing-country verification. If your card's BIN (the first 6 digits) comes from a restricted country, the payment is automatically declined.

This isn't a bug. It's enforcement of international sanctions and FIFA's own ticket restrictions.

But here's the thing: Visa and Mastercard enforce these rules. Cryptocurrency doesn't.

## How Crypto Fixes It

Cryptocurrency transactions don't check:
- Your nationality
- Your country of residence
- Your bank's location
- Whether your government is sanctioned

A Bitcoin transaction from a Russian wallet looks exactly the same as one from a Swiss wallet. There's no issuing country. There's no BIN lookup. There's no one to say no.

## The Process

1. **Get a wallet.** Any crypto wallet works. I recommend one that supports USDT (Tether) since the value is stable.
2. **Buy USDT or BTC.** Use a local exchange or a peer-to-peer platform. Some clients use Dubai exchanges. Others use Paxful or Binance P2P.
3. **Send to the concierge.** They give you a wallet address and the exact amount. The transaction confirms in 10-30 minutes.
4. **You're confirmed.** Your package is secured. No bank involvement.

## The Countries Where Crypto Is the Only Option

Based on actual bookings:

- **Russia:** 40+ clients this tournament. Almost all use USDT.
- **Iran:** 12 clients. All crypto.
- **Venezuela:** 5 clients. BTC or USDT.
- **North Korea:** 3 clients. Gold-backed crypto.
- **Sudan, Syria, Yemen:** Various. Always crypto.

## Common Questions

**Is it safe?** Yes. The concierge provides a verified wallet address. Transactions are recorded on the blockchain. You can track the confirmation yourself.

**What if I send the wrong amount?** The concierge confirms receipt within minutes. Overpayments are refunded in the same cryptocurrency.

**Do I need to be a tech person?** No. The concierge walks you through it. Even my father managed it.

## The Bottom Line

If your bank card works internationally: use it.

If it doesn't — and you know who you are — cryptocurrency is the workaround.

The concierge team accepts BTC, ETH, USDT, USDC, and SOL. Every single one bypasses the banking restrictions that FIFA's checkout enforces.

[Pay with crypto] — select it as your method when you book.

*This isn't a crypto ad. It's a practical solution for people whose banks won't let them enjoy the World Cup.*
